title: Finance Briefing: This bond strategy can protect your portfolio even if interest rates go up
summary: Leo Stone maps the latest market headlines to liquidity, rates, earnings expectations, and risk appetite.

body.text: The useful way to read market news is to connect each headline to a mechanism: cash flows, liquidity, policy expectations, positioning, or risk appetite.

items: This bond strategy can protect your portfolio even if interest rates go up
Kevin Warsh walks into a trap where the Fed can’t cut rates even if it wants to
Goldman Sachs notes shift in fund positioning towards semis and away from software
‘My husband is leery of my plan’: We are both 60 and have $5 million. Is now a good time to dip into our savings?
‘He didn’t really pay attention’: I told my friend he left millions on the table in retirement. Did I do the right thing?

body.text: The first-order story is rarely enough. A professional reading asks whether the news changes the discount rate, the earnings path, the cost of capital, or the market's willingness to pay for uncertainty.

body.alt: Image from source article for Kevin Warsh walks into a trap where the Fed can’t cut rates even if it wants to

body.sourceTitle: Kevin Warsh walks into a trap where the Fed can’t cut rates even if it wants to

body.text: This article is informational analysis, not investment advice. The goal is to make the signal clearer and the assumptions more explicit.
